body.title: Verify pe_chain work routes to cheap tier (Qwen) not interactive (Claude)
body.result: already correct (route('batch') returns qwen2.5:7b); added _log_pe_inference() so model id is logged to reasoning_calls.log on every _call_tier2/_call_cloud_programming success; added integration test tests/test_pe_chain_qwen_tier.py verifying logged model contains 'qwen' not 'claude'
body.description: Qwen infrastructure already in place: inference_gateway tier ladder has 'tier.2 Ollama qwen2.5:7b — background, NE, batch' (inference_gateway.py:42), Qwen-2.5-Coder pulled on CPU boxes (T-pull-qwen-coder done), OR routing exists (reading extraction uses qwen/qwen-2.5-7b-instruct). What's unverified: does pe_chain request the cheap background tier when processing tickets, or does it default to interactive (Claude)? Fix shape: inspect pe_chain's reasoner call sites, confirm they request batch/background tier. If they default to interactive, add an explicit tier hint so ticket processing stays cheap. Ensure turn_trace logs which model actually answered so cost is auditable on demand. Scope: pe_chain tier hints + turn_trace visibility. Does not touch inference_gateway internals. Does not add new models. Does not touch reasoners/base.py (HIGH-inertia). Test plan: run one worker=igor ticket through pe_chain, grep turn_trace for model id, confirm Qwen. If Claude: inspect call site, add tier hint. Akien 2026-04-20 constraint: if Qwen quality is insufficient, next step is larger-param Qwen, not fallback to Claude.
